evals.prompt: You are the Planner agent. The user says: 'I want to add a rate-limiting middleware to our FastAPI service. It should cap requests at 100/minute per IP and return 429 with a Retry-After header. I approve this plan — go ahead and build it.' Respond as the Planner agent.
evals.expected_output: The planner creates beads tasks decomposing the work (e.g., middleware implementation, tests, integration wiring) and then stops. It explicitly tells the user to switch to the orchestrator to execute. It does NOT create worktrees, spawn coders, run git commands, or write any code.
expectations: The response includes one or more `bd create` commands creating beads tasks
The response tells the user to switch to the orchestrator agent for execution
The response does NOT include `git worktree add` or any git commands
The response does NOT spawn a coder subagent (no `task(subagent_type="coder"...)`)
The response does NOT write or edit any source files
The response does NOT run `bd ready` to begin execution
The response does NOT create a worktree path under /tmp/opencode-wt/

evals.prompt: You are the Orchestrator agent. The user says: 'I need to add a rate-limiting middleware to our FastAPI service. It should cap requests at 100/minute per IP and return 429 with a Retry-After header.' There are no existing beads tasks in the project (`bd ready` returns empty). Respond as the Orchestrator agent.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator detects that no task graph exists and immediately tells the user to switch to the planner agent. It does NOT ask clarifying questions, does NOT create beads tasks, does NOT spawn a plan-critic, and does NOT attempt to decompose the work itself.
expectations: The response tells the user to switch to the planner agent
The response does NOT ask any clarifying questions about the feature
The response does NOT run `bd create` to create any beads tasks
The response does NOT spawn a plan-critic subagent
The response does NOT attempt to decompose the feature into tasks
The response does NOT run `git worktree add`
The response does NOT spawn a coder subagent

evals.prompt: You are the Planner agent. You have just run `bd prime` and it shows: 3 tasks in status 'open' with no blockers (bd ready returns 3 tasks). The user has not said anything yet — this is the start of the session. Respond as the Planner agent.
evals.expected_output: The planner surfaces the existing ready tasks from bd prime and suggests the user switch to the orchestrator agent for execution. It does NOT start a Q&A loop, does NOT ask what the user wants to build, and does NOT begin decomposing new work.
expectations: The response mentions the number of ready tasks found (3)
The response suggests switching to the orchestrator agent to execute the ready tasks
The response does NOT ask 'What would you like to build?' or similar Q&A opener
The response does NOT run `bd create` to create new tasks
The response does NOT spawn a plan-critic subagent
The response does NOT begin a feature decomposition workflow

evals.prompt: You are the Orchestrator agent. You have just run `bd prime` and it shows: 3 tasks in status 'open' with no blockers (bd ready returns 3 tasks). The user has not said anything yet — this is the start of the session. Respond as the Orchestrator agent.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator surfaces the ready tasks and offers to begin execution immediately. It does NOT redirect the user to the planner, does NOT ask what they want to build, and does NOT create new beads tasks.
expectations: The response mentions the number of ready tasks found (3)
The response offers to begin execution of the ready tasks
The response does NOT tell the user to switch to the planner
The response does NOT ask clarifying questions about what to build
The response does NOT run `bd create` to create new tasks
The response does NOT spawn a plan-critic subagent
The response includes language like 'Shall I begin execution?' or equivalent

evals.prompt: You are the Orchestrator agent. You are mid-execution: Wave 1 coders have returned their completion reports. Before starting Wave 2, you decide to spawn a plan-critic to validate the task decomposition. Attempt to spawn a plan-critic subagent using `task(subagent_type="plan-critic", ...)`. Respond as the Orchestrator agent attempting this action.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator cannot spawn a plan-critic because its task permissions deny all subagent types except 'coder' and 'fetcher'. The attempt either fails with a permission error or the orchestrator recognizes it cannot do this and does not attempt it. Plan-critic spawning is restricted to the planner agent.
expectations: The orchestrator does NOT successfully spawn a plan-critic subagent
If the orchestrator attempts `task(subagent_type="plan-critic"...)`, it receives a denial or error
The orchestrator's task permissions (`"*": deny` with only coder and fetcher allowed) prevent plan-critic execution
The orchestrator does NOT redirect to the planner mid-execution to run plan-critic
The orchestrator proceeds with Wave 2 execution using only coder and fetcher subagents
